title: Install dtwiz - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand how the dtwiz CLI is installed.

questions.title: What does the install one-liner do?
questions.content: In Section 01 you ran `source <(curl -sSL .../scripts/install.sh)`. What does it do?

options.text: Detects your platform, downloads the right dtwiz binary, and puts it on your PATH
options.explanation: The official installer resolves OS + architecture and installs to /usr/local/bin or ~/bin.

options.text: Compiles dtwiz from Go sources on your machine
options.explanation: Building from source is possible (make install) but the one-liner downloads a prebuilt release binary.
dtwiz is the CLI that can LATER install ingestion methods like OneAgent - the installer only installs dtwiz itself.

questions.explanation: The install script downloads the prebuilt dtwiz release for your platform and adds it to your PATH.
hints: It finishes in seconds - too fast for a compile.
Check where the binary landed with `command -v dtwiz`.

questions.title: Why source the installer?
questions.content: The recommended form is `source <(curl ...)` instead of `curl ... | sh`. Why?

options.text: Sourcing makes dtwiz available immediately in the current terminal (PATH updated in-place)
options.explanation: Piping to sh runs in a subshell - you would need a new terminal for PATH changes to apply.

options.text: Piping to sh is blocked by GitHub
options.explanation: Piping works fine - it is listed as the alternative in the script header.

options.text: Sourcing verifies the download's checksum, piping does not
options.explanation: The download behaves the same either way - the difference is only the shell the PATH export runs in.

questions.explanation: source runs the installer in YOUR shell, so its PATH update takes effect immediately - no new terminal needed.
hints: Think about which shell the `export PATH=...` runs in.

learningObjectives: Install the dtwiz CLI with the official installer.
